layers.label: Programs, waivers, appeals, forms, and waitlists
layers.gap: Program foundation is strong, but waitlist depth is still smaller than the rest of the program layer.

layers.label: County offices, DD routing, and education routing
layers.gap: Routing layers are broadly populated, but density and local depth still vary by state and county.

layers.label: Local nonprofit support organizations
layers.gap: Nonprofit coverage is broad, but foundation metadata like accessibility and live capacity is still sparse.

layers.label: IEP advocates and advocacy support
layers.gap: Advocate count is strong, but truth quality and public eligibility still need to stay under active audit.

layers.label: Clinics, therapists, and local providers
evidence: 23 staged provider rows waiting in provider staging
layers.gap: Provider coverage is the clearest national information gap. This layer exists, but it is nowhere near all-state depth.

layers.label: Findhelp-like metadata on directory listings
evidence: Schema support exists for tags, availability, next steps, accessibility, claims, and trust fields on providers, nonprofits, and advocates.
Those fields sit on 33111 directory rows total.
layers.gap: The metadata model exists, but live availability, accessibility, and capacity signals are still sparse on the checked-in DB.

layers.label: Organization -> program -> location normalization
layers.gap: This migration landing zone is modeled in schema but not yet populated in the checked-in DB.

layers.label: Conditions, functional needs, and reference knowledge
layers.gap: Core condition and need knowledge is populated, but some supporting reference tables are still empty in the checked-in DB.

layers.label: Knowledge articles and guidance content
layers.gap: Knowledge content exists, but article volume is still small relative to the breadth of the rest of the information model.

layers.label: Family, case, and navigator-adjacent workflow data
layers.gap: The schema supports family/case workflows, but the checked-in DB mostly shows demo-level or empty runtime data.

layers.label: User submissions, coverage gaps, and feedback loops
layers.gap: These operational loops are modeled but not populated in the checked-in DB snapshot.

conclusion.reason: The repo has strong national program, routing, nonprofit, advocate, and condition foundations, but provider coverage, normalization, knowledge depth, and workflow/runtime layers are still thin or empty in the checked-in DB.
